title: Sanguinaria ciliaris
taxonomy: Sanguinaria ciliaris is a species of flowering plant, but it is actually not classified as Sanguinaria ciliaris, it is actually classified as Paeonia ciliata. This classification is based on the most recent and widely accepted botanical research and studies. Paeonia ciliata is a member of the Paeoniaceae family, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as peonies. The Paeoniaceae family is part of the larger order Ranunculales, which includes other flowering plants such as ranunculuses and aquilegias. This classification is used in modern botanical and horticultural contexts.
uses: Sanguinaria ciliaris, also known as the yellow adder root, has been traditionally used in folk medicine. The plant's rhizomes and roots have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In traditional Chinese medicine, Sanguinaria ciliaris is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. However, its use is not without controversy, as some studies suggest it may be toxic in large quantities. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential risks associated with Sanguinaria ciliaris.
benefits: Sanguinaria ciliaris, also known as yellow sanguinaria or yellow bloodroot, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce swelling and fight off infections. The plant's roots contain a compound called sanguinarine, which has been studied for its potential to have antioxidant and anticancer effects. Some traditional remedies use the plant to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential health benefits and any potential side effects.
properties: Sanguinaria ciliaris has been traditionally used in Eastern medicine to treat various health conditions. Its roots and rhizomes are believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant is said to have a stimulating effect on the immune system and is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. It is also used to treat digestive problems, including diarrhea and indigestion. However, its effectiveness and safety as a treatment have not been extensively scientifically proven.
constituents: Sanguinaria ciliaris, also known as yellow sanguinaria, contains various active constituents. These include alkaloids, specifically sanguinarine, dihydrosanguinarine, and chelerythrine. Additionally, the plant contains protopine and other benzophenanthridine alkaloids. These compounds have been found to have potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. However, the exact composition and concentration of these constituents can vary depending on factors such as the plant's habitat and growing conditions.
parts: Sanguinaria ciliaris, commonly known as yellow puccoon, is a plant native to North America. The medicinal parts of this plant are primarily its rhizome and root, which are rich in alkaloids. These compounds have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The rhizome and root of Sanguinaria ciliaris contain sanguinarine and dihydrochelerythrine, which are the primary bioactive compounds responsible for its medicinal properties. These compounds have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making the plant a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
preparations: Sanguinaria ciliaris is often used in traditional herbal preparations, particularly in Chinese medicine. It's believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are utilized to treat various health issues. The plant's roots and rhizomes are typically harvested and processed into extracts or powders for medicinal use. Sanguinaria ciliaris is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects. However, its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and side effects.
side_effects: Sanguinaria ciliaris, also known as Sanguinaria canadensis, contains compounds that may cause side effects due to its toxicity. These compounds, including glycosides and alkaloids, can cause symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea when ingested in large quantities. Ingesting the plant can also lead to more severe side effects, including seizures, tremors, and respiratory failure in extreme cases. Additionally, Sanguinaria ciliaris can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. It is essential to handle the plant with caution and avoid ingesting it altogether.
intro: Sanguinaria ciliaris, also known as yellow sanguinaria, is a perennial plant belonging to the poppy family (Papaveraceae) that is native to Eastern North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Sanguinaria ciliaris include sanguinarine, a benzylisoquinoline alkaloid with analgesic and sedative properties, as well as other alkaloids like chelerythrine and dihydrosanguinarine. In herbal medicine, Sanguinaria ciliaris is commonly prepared as a tincture, syrup, or dried root, which is used to treat various ailments, although its use should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to potential toxicity.
